title: palms cafe putrajaya christmas new year feast
full_text: Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Main Eat Drink Reviews Insider At Home About Us Download Our Magazine Q2 2026 Q1 2026 Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026 Now Reading: 2025 Christmas In Style, By the Palms Café, Palm Garden Hotel Putrajaya, Your Christmas & New Year Feast Awaits 1 01 2025 Christmas In Style, By the Palms Café, Palm Garden Hotel Putrajaya, Your Christmas & New Year Feast Awaits Main// Eat// Drink// Reviews// Insider// At Home// About Us// Download Our Magazine// Q2 2026// Q1 2026// Vol 1 Palate Dining Guide 2025-2026// Home Reviews 2025 Christmas In Style, By the Palms Café, Palm Garden Hotel Putrajaya, Your Christmas & New Year Feast Awaits2025 Christmas In Style, By the Palms Café, Palm Garden Hotel Putrajaya, Your Christmas & New Year Feast AwaitsVanessa LEat, Reviews, Events6 months agoShare Get ready for Christmas in three different locations. Christmas is coming, this either means Santa is bearing presents or grandma is plating up a festive feast—or both. Somewhere in Putrajaya, a mere steer away from IOI City Mall, resides Palm Garden Hotel Putrajaya. Some may see it a trace quiet, others like myself imagine a rather serene hideaway. Picture traditional Malaysian artistry breathing in each corner you stroll along or slip into a sun-lit courtyard with a fountain humming tranquil tunes as you tend to work. Brewing inside? An all-day platter paradise. Firing up tasty à la carte menu as well as a fetching local and international melting pot, Palms Café doesn’t turn its nose up at fusion fares, and that’s the type of allure this restaurant reserves. As Christmas lights begin to brighten, weather tumbling into a humid haze, Palms Café steps into the shoes as a host, welcoming you to an eat that spreads joy, and satiates appetites. Festive Eve Dinner On 24 December 2025, embarks the Festive Eve Dinner—whether for family feasting together, a couple’s treat, or one-persons tables, this is a yuletide meant to be spent and shared here at Palms Café, Putrajaya. Nothing too elaborate, just enthusiasm in casual warmth. Winging the spotlight with Western spread, the Oven-Baked Whole Giant Chicken—with all the trimmings—a delectable bird in the merry bunch. Those with a tide of taste, the Baked Salmon! Golden-crusted, richly flavoured flesh, each forkful just a lemon-capers sauce away from bliss. A pine for red protein? The Baked Beef Tenderloin—a succulent stunner, ringing a holiday taste. Tender muscles draped in bronzed coat, think rich meat and a rustic edge with each tear, it’s a bite that sleighs. A local affair There’s nothing more dazzling than a turn with a local affair. Spinning into the merry scene with a tang to its vibrancy, the Asam Pedas Ekor. Its distinct flavour profile outshines others as oxtail takes the leading bite in this dish. Slow-cooked until a boneless surrender, the balance between a sour-spicy smack counts delicately on the sweetness traced in shallots or gingers. Traditional? Yes. But a timeless special, nonetheless. The Portuguese-style Ikan Bakar might just be a worthy challenge to the Christmas crown, think butterflied white fish under a savoury-spicy sambal blanket. Encased in banana leaves before its toasty sleep over charcoal. The butterflying technique shortens cooking time while opening room for flavours to permeate fully. Echoing Portuguese culinary craft with said style, what is in the delicious act is ingredients like lemongrass and shrimp paste, too. Besides the many, one more nod to our local palate is the Nestum Prawns. Some call it cereal prawns, and as its cheeky name suggests, it’s deep-fried prawns tossed in crispy cereal mixture. Rather a celebratory crunch between spicy-sweet sizzle, an instant cereal—Nestum, along with curry leaves and bird’s eye chillies are the secret spices to this dish. Hot & happening: slurp and snap! For a less dry and more moist spoonfuls, the noodle counter is yours to explore. Offering broths next to noodle line-ups, brewing luscious assam laksa ambrosia or clear soup or prawn noodles; the tasty twirls stretch to pastas as well. Endless selections, even better a saucy slurp. Or a snap that refreshes? Salad stations are at your fingertips to farm and fold. Decadent Christmas darlings Christmas Fruit Cake Spoiling the sweet-toothed, Palms Café bears a sweet sensation station. Because some nights—or a jubilant one like this night, is meant to end with and only with decadent delights! Picture a Christmas Fruit Cake, soaked in spirits, studded with nuts, spices, and dried fruits. Or the Durian Meringue Tart—bold but bite-sized riches. Lounging next to this is the Yule Log Cake, petite Pecan Tarts, and a sweet medley beyond! Christmas is coming! Nigh is New Year! On the very holly day stay at Palm Garden Hotel Putrajaya, breakfast? Optional. High-tea time, however, now that’s the treasure to sip to. Let noon simmer as you indulge in curated confits, available for RM118 per adult. Let evening tiptoe in, and Christmas carollers jingle by, chiming with holiday joy on the Festive Eve as well as Festive Day buffets. @palmgardenputrajaya Tuning into 2026, with a spectacular salute to 31 December 2025, Palms Café welcomes you to a beautiful buffet spread accenting Roasted Lamb Leg, ravishing Beef Ribs, Grilled Prawns, and Mussel Au Gratin as a delicious appetiser. Mixing in the merry charm are local gems and global treasures. Twirling before you, live custom noodle creations as well as a pasta post to meal with starters, smooth soups, and decadent desserts. Sprouting joy with the Children’s Corner where your kiddies can roam and roar about, with Mr. Clown to share and spread the cheers. Grown-ups? Groove on over to an in-house DJ alongside live band sets. And as midnight sneaks by, enjoy a private fireworks performance—bottoms up to the New Year in style! buffetchristmasnew yearShare Leave a reply Cancel reply Save my name, email, and website in this browser for the next time I comment.
